segments.s500-g172: ز بهر is a causal marker combination meaning 'because of / on account of'. فزونی 'excess, surplus' is the reason; به تنگ اندریم literally 'we are to narrowness' i.e. 'we are pressed into narrowness' or 'we are cramped.' اندریم again is the archaic 1st plural copular/locative form 'we are / we find ourselves'.
segments.s500-g173: ز چندین 'of/from many' introduces the group; یکی را uses یکی as 'one (person)' with را as the definite/object marker/emphatic. نبودست is an older/perfective negative 'there was not / did not exist'. شوی (from شدن/شو) here functions as 'become / take (as a husband)' — the whole clause: 'of many, there was not one who became (the one)'.
segments.s500-g174: که introduces an explanatory clause 'for/because'. دوشیزگانیم is a 1st‑person plural present copula 'we are maidens'. و is the coordinating 'and'. پوشیده‌روی is an adjectival epithet 'veiled/modest‑faced' giving the reason why none 'became' — i.e. 'because we are maidens and modest in face.'
segments.s500-g175: ز هر سو که آیی is a circumstantial clause 'from every side/wherever you come'. برین بوم is a compound locative: بر (on/upon) + ین (the demonstrative/orthographic 'this') + بوم (land/homeland) → 'this land/on this land'. The final بر begins the next clause ('and on/onto...').
segments.s500-g176: بجز introduces an exception 'except/apart from'. ژرف دریا 'the deep sea' is that exception. نبینی is 2nd‑person singular present negative 'you do not see'; گذر is 'passage/way'. Together: 'you see no passage except the deep sea.'
segments.s500-g177: ز ما (literally “from us”) is a poetic way to say “among us” or “of us.” هر زنی is the indefinite phrase “any woman” (هر = any/every; زنی = woman). کو is an archaic/poetic relative particle here meaning “who/that” (literally ‘where’ in other uses). گراید is a classical-present/poetic form of گراییدن “to turn” (i.e. “turns” or “turns away”). بشوی is an archaic/poetic form corresponding to بشود / خواهد شد (“will be” / “becomes”) used here after the relative clause to complete the idea — so the clause reads “any woman of ours who turns (away) (will be [so]).”
segments.s500-g178: ازان پس means “from that time on” / “thereafter.” کس here, combined with the negative verb that follows, functions as “no one” (the noun is then negated by the verb). او را uses the classical object-marking را to mark “her” as the definite object. نه‌بینیم is negation (نه-) plus the first-person-plural present subjunctive/indicative form of دیدن (بینیم) used here to express the negative result (“we do not / will not see”). روی is “face.” Together: “thereafter no one will see her face.”
segments.s500-g179: بباید is a modal auxiliary meaning “one must / it is necessary.” It is followed by the infinitive گذشتن “to pass / to cross,” so بباید + infinitive = “must cross.” به introduces the goal or direction (“into/toward”), and دریای ژرف is the object phrase “the deep sea” (دریای = sea + genitive marker, ژرف = deep).
segments.s500-g180: اگر خوش is an abbreviated conditional clause “if (it is) fair/pleasant” where the copula is omitted (typical in poetry). و‌گر means “and if / but if” linking an alternative condition; نیز = “also / even.” باریده is a perfect-participle form meaning “has fallen/has rained” and here takes برف (“snow”) as its subject; together باریده برف = “snow has fallen.” The line therefore gives two conditions under which one must cross the deep sea: whether it is fine or even if snow has fallen.
segments.s500-g181: اگر introduces a conditional “if.” دختر here means “maiden” (classical sense) rather than merely “daughter.” آیدش is the fused form of آمد + ضمیرِ مَضاف (آید + -ش) meaning “comes to him / arrives for him.” چون is “as / like.” کردشوی is an archaic/poetic second-person perfect/perfective-form meaning roughly “you have made / you have fashioned” (literally کرد + شوی in classical usage), so the clause reads “if a maiden comes to him as you have made/fashioned (her).”
segments.s500-g182: This is a descriptive nominal phrase: زن‌آسا is an adjectival compound meaning “woman-like / delicate/feminine.” و is the coordinator “and.” جوینده is a present-participial adjective meaning “seeking” or “desirous of.” رنگ here means “complexion/appearance” (i.e. adornment/make-up) rather than simple ‘paint,’ and بوی is “scent.” Together: “womanly and seeking color (complexion/ornament) and scent,” describing the maiden’s feminine, adornment-seeking character.
segments.s500-g183: هم is an emphatic “also/indeed.” آن خانه جاوید is a noun phrase “that eternal house” (خانه = house, جاوید = eternal). جای وی است is a copular clause: جای = “place,” وی = possessive/third-person (“his/her”), and است is the copula “is.” Word order is the classical Persian pattern where the predicate follows the subject phrase: “That eternal house is his place.”
segments.s500-g184: This line uses inverted/parallel phrasing. بلند qualifies آسمانش: “lofty/high is its sky.” آسمانش = آسمان + possessive suffix (“its/its sky”). هوای وی است is another copular clause: هوای = “the air / atmosphere/breeze,” وی = “his,” and است = “is,” i.e. “its air is his.” The two clauses together praise the place as lofty and belonging to him.
segments.s500-g273: This is a noun phrase: همه (all) modifies دیده‌هاشان (their eyes — دیده + possessive -شان). به کردار is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'in the manner of' or 'like' (به + کردار, where کردار is used here as 'manner/way'), so the whole segment means 'all their eyes like blood.' segments.s500-g274: A verbal clause with the old continuous particle همی (here marking an ongoing or repeated action) plus verb آمد (came). از دهان = 'from the mouth' (از = from), آتش = 'fire' is the subject/thing that آمد (came), and برون = 'out' is a post-verbal adverb meaning 'out/forth.' segments.s500-g275: Simple past clause: بسی (an intensive/quantifying word meaning 'much/many' here) modifies پیل (elephants). بردند is the past-tense verb 'they carried/brought'; پیشش = 'before him/in his presence' (پیش + -ش), and به راه = 'on the road/along the way' (به + راه). segments.s500-g278: A compact clause: ز ما بود = 'was from us' or 'originated with us' (ز = from, بود = was), کامد is an archaic present/imperative-like form meaning 'comes/will come' (here future/inevitable sense), شما را (marked object) = 'to you,' and زیان = 'harm/detriment.' segments.s500-g9: This is a short, poetic clause meaning “the army came/advanced with arrows and swords.” The two prepositional phrases 'به تیر و به تیغ' use به instrumentally (‘by/with’), repeated for emphasis; 'اندر آمد' is a classical compound verb meaning ‘came in/advanced’ (اندر + آمد) with the verb preceding its subject 'سپاه' (a common epic inversion).
segments.s500-g10: 'تو گفتی' is the 2nd-person past ('you said'); here 'گفتی' functions as a past-tense reporting verb (note: the provided lexical senses were for a noun form, so no matching verbal sense is listed). segments.s500-g11: چو here is a temporal conjunction meaning ‘when’; 'از' is partitive ('of/from'), so 'از نرم‌پایان فراوان' = ‘many of the light‑footed.’ 'بماند' (a form of ماندن) functions here as a past narrative/resultative ‘remained/was left’ (classical narrative forms can use this shape). segments.s500-g12: This is two coordinated verb phrases about the subject (implicit or topical): 'سکندر برآسود' — 'Alexander rested/was at ease' (برآسود is a compound perfective/state verb meaning became at rest/relieved) — and 'و لشکر براند' — 'and (he) drove off/put to flight the host' (راند/براند used in the past sense). segments.s500-g14: که introduces a relative clause modifying 'شهری'. 'آن را' is a demonstrative object referring back to the city (آن with the object-marker را). segments.s500-g16: Two coordinated adjectives 'گشاده‌دل و بی‌نیاز' describe the manner of their coming: 'generous/open‑hearted and carefree/self‑sufficient.' The final 'آمدند' repeats the verbal action for emphasis (they came in that generous, untroubled fashion).
segments.s500-g89: 'وزان جایگه' is a compact classical phrase meaning 'from that place' (وز = from/and from + ان = demonstrative); 'تیز لشکر' places the adjective 'تیز' (swift) before the noun 'لشکر' (host/army). The verb 'براند' is an older/epic-form of راندن (3rd person singular) used narratively here — translate as past 'departed' or 'drove off'; no expressed object is required (the host is the subject).
segments.s500-g90: 'تن اژدها را' is a definite object phrase ('تن' = body + genitive 'اژدها' = of the dragon) followed by the object marker 'را', placed before the verb as is normal in Persian narrative. 'هم‌انجا' = 'at that same place/there as well'. 'بماند' is the epic/present-form of ماندن (here best translated past: 'remained' or 'was left').
segments.s500-g91: The verb 'بیاورد' (brought/carried) appears first in this classical word order; the agent 'لشکر' follows it. The prepositional phrase 'به کوهی دگر' = 'to another/a different mountain' uses 'کوهی' (an indefinite or adjectival form related to 'کوه') with 'دگر' ('other').
segments.s500-g92: 'کزان' = 'from which' (referring back to the mountain) introduces where the following action is located. 'خیره شد' is a compound with 'خیره' (staring/fixed) + 'شد' (became) — a standard past narrative meaning 'stood transfixed/was staring'. 'مرد پرخاشخر' (the man + epithet) follows the verb as the subject in this poetic word order: 'the quarrelsome man was staring.'
segments.s500-g93: 'بلندیش' = بلند + possessive/relational ending (his/its height/loftiness); 'بینا' here functions like 'visible/clearly seen' or 'with sight'; 'همی دیر' is an adverbial cluster meaning 'for a long while' ('همی' emphatic/still + 'دیر' long (of time)). The verb 'دید' (saw) closes the clause — classical Persian often places descriptive phrases before the verb.
segments.s500-g94: 'سر کوه' = 'the summit/top of the mountain' (سر = top, followed by the noun it modifies). 'چون' = 'like/as' introducing a simile; 'تیغ و شمشیر' = 'sword and blade' (two images reinforcing sharpness). The clause ends with the repeated verb 'دید' (saw), meaning 'he saw (it) as ...'.
segments.s500-g95: 'یکی' = 'a certain' (introducing an item); 'تخت زرین' = 'golden throne' (adjective after noun is possible but here 'زرین' follows تخت and qualifies it). 'بران' is an older/poetic form meaning 'upon that' (بر + آن compressed). 'تیغ کوه' = 'the mountain's blade/ridge' (tiġ as a metaphorical sharp ridge). The phrase describes location: 'a certain golden throne on that mountain-blade.'
segments.s500-g96: 'ز' is the poetic/prepositional 'from' (ز = از); 'انبوه' here refers to the mass/crowd (literally 'multitude/dense'); 'یکسو' = 'to one side' (indicating separation); the coordinating 'و' links the next descriptor 'دور از گروه' = 'distant from the company/troop' (دور = far, از گروه = from the group). Together the phrase says the throne was set aside from the throng, to one side and far from the troop.
segments.s500-g65: بکشت is an archaic past 3rd‑person form (‘he killed’/‘he slew’); و simply coordinates the clauses. ز introduces the source/part (‘from their heads’), سرشان is the possessed noun ‘their head(s)’, and برآهخت is a past (3sg) perfective verb ‘lifted/raised’ (bar‑āhukht) that takes پوست (‘skin’) as its object. Word order is flexible/classical Persian: the prepositional source (ز سرشان) precedes the verb, and the verb governs the object پوست.
segments.s500-g66: بدان here functions as a compound prepositional/adverbial phrase meaning ‘by/with that’ (literally built from ب + دان) linking to the previous action. جادوی داده is a noun cluster ‘the magic that was given’ (جادوی = magic, داده = given) and دل مرد دوست is a genitive chain ‘the heart of the beloved man’ (دل = heart; مرد = man; دوست = beloved/favourite). The whole segment reads: ‘by that given magic (he) [affecting] the heart of the beloved man.’
segments.s500-g67: بیاگند is a (narrative) past or causative verb meaning ‘he made to rot/putrefy’ or more generally ‘he soaked/treated’ here; چرمش is the direct object with the 3rd‑person possessive suffix ‘his hide/leather’. به … و به … uses the preposition به repeated for each instrument/agent: ‘with poison and with oil’. The clause means he treated his leather with poison and oil.
segments.s500-g68: سوی expresses direction ‘toward(s)’, اژدها is the object of that direction ‘the dragon’, and روی here is the specific target ‘the face / surface’. بنهاد is a past 3sg verb ‘he placed/put’ (often used for setting or applying something), and تفت is used as the action/result ‘burning/scorching’. Together: ‘he set (it) toward the dragon’s face to scorch it.’
segments.s500-g69: مران is a proper name (the agent). چرمها را is the definite direct object (چرمها = the hides; را marks them as definite/accusative). پر از باد کرد is a causative phrase: پر از = ‘full of’, باد = ‘wind’, and کرد is the past 3sg ‘made/did’ — ‘he made the hides full of wind’ (i.e., he inflated them).
segments.s500-g70: ز here is a preposition meaning ‘from/with/regarding’ connecting the following noun to the remembrance. دادار is an epithet for the Creator/Giver (God). نیکی دهش is a noun phrase ‘gift/grant of goodness’ (نیکی = goodness; دهش = gift/grant). یاد کرد is the common compound meaning ‘he remembered’ or ‘he gave remembrance’ (یاد as notion of memory + کرد as the past auxiliary/verb). Together: ‘he remembered the Creator’s gift of goodness.’
segments.s500-g71: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past 3sg form ‘he commanded/ordered/said’. تا introduces a subordinate clause of purpose/result ‘so that / until’, here giving the content of the order. پوست is the object ‘skins’, and برداشتند is past 3rd‑person plural ‘they removed/took off’. segments.s500-g72: همی is an adverb meaning ‘continually/at once’ in narrative style. دست بر دست is an idiomatic phrase literally ‘hand upon hand’; with بگذاشتند (past 3pl ‘they placed/put’) it describes them immediately setting to work or joining hands — here it conveys that they set about the task together. بگذاشتند is the past 3rd‑person plural verb agreeing with the implied agents.
segments.s500-g17: ببردند is a past plural verb 'they took/carried off' (classical past). هرگونه is a premodifier meaning 'every kind of' and precedes the following noun. گستردنی is a deverbal/adjectival noun (literally 'that which can be spread' or 'spreadable things'); the whole noun phrase هرگونه گستردنی is the object of ببردند.
segments.s500-g18: This is a coordinated pair of prepositional/source phrases. ز (poetic variant of از) and از both mark origin/source ('from'). پوشیدنیها is the plural noun 'garments/clothes' (–ها plural). و simply coordinates, and خوردنی is used as a noun 'food/edible things' (originally an adjective but nominalized here).
segments.s500-g19: سکندر is the subject (Alexander). بپرسید is a past tense verb 'he asked/inquired' (classical narrative past). The conjunction و links the next verb. بنواختشان is past 'he caused to sound / played' (نواختن) with the object suffix -شان 'them' attached (i.e. 'had them play' or 'made music for them').
segments.s500-g20: براندازه is an adverbial/phrase 'in proportion / to the proper measure' modifying the action. بر is the preposition 'on / upon', and پایگه is the location noun 'platform / base'; ساختشان is past 'he made/arranged them' with the object suffix -شان. Word order places the measure-adverb and locative before the verb, a common poetic/old Persian ordering.
segments.s500-g21: کشیدند is past plural 'they stretched/erected' (here used for putting up tents/pavilions). بر دشت is a locative prepositional phrase 'on the plain' (بر = on, دشت = plain). پرده‌سرای is the direct object ('pavilion/curtain-hall / tented hall' or theatrical pavilion) that they erected.
segments.s500-g22: سپاهش is 'سپاه' (army) plus the possessive clitic 'ش' = 'his army'. نجست is the past of جستن 'to seek' and here functions idiomatically as 'did not find' / 'could not find' (an archaic usage in narrative). اندر آن شهر جای is a locative phrase 'in that city (there was) place' — جای is the noun 'place' serving as the thing sought by the army.
segments.s500-g23: This is a poetic word order. سر ('summit/top') followed by اندر ستاره 'among the stars' locates that summit. یکی کوه is 'a certain / one mountain' (یکی used as an indefinite 'a certain'), and دید is the past verb 'he saw'. The modifiers precede the noun and verb in classical style.
segments.s500-g24: تو گفتی is the reporting clause 'you said' (note: the provided sense list for گفتی does not include the verbal sense 'you said', so that specific lexical sense is not listed below). که introduces the reported content clause. گردون is 'the sky/heavens' and بخواهد is a modal/future auxiliary 'would/will want' (here marking future/intent). کشید (here) is the main verb 'to pull/drag' in the reported clause meaning 'would draw/pull (it)'.
segments.s500-g233: چو is a conditional/temporal conjunction here meaning 'if/when'. دیدار is a noun 'meeting'. باشد is the subjunctive/present form of باش (here functioning as 'there is / there will be'). برانم is the first-person singular form of راندن with the subjunctive/jussive ending -م and here expresses 'I will drive away / I will send off'. سپاه is the direct object 'army, host' and follows the verb in this poetic word order.
segments.s500-g234: نباشم is the negated first-person form of بودن (نباشم = 'I will not be / I shall not remain') with the sense of not remaining or being present. فراوان here qualifies the verb and means 'in great numbers / abundantly'. بدین جایگاه is a prepositional phrase (ب + این جایگاه) meaning 'in/to this place' (here rendered 'here'); the provided tokenization split ب and دین, but دین in this context does not align with the listed senses so it is marked -1.
segments.s500-g235: ببینیم is the first-person plural subjunctive/jussive of دیدن meaning 'let us see / we shall see'. تا here introduces the purpose/indirect question 'so that / in order to' or simply 'to' in the sense of 'let us see what...'. چیستتان combines چیست ('what is') with the enclitic تان ('your') and asks 'what is yours'. رای and فر are coordinated nouns 'counsel/plan' and 'far/royal glory' that form the question's content.
segments.s500-g236: This segment is a nominal list joined by و. سواری is a substantive meaning 'rider(s) / cavalry' or 'horsemen' (here a group/quality). زیبای + ی is split in the tokenization: زیبای functions as 'beautiful' or a stem of 'beauty', and the separate ی functions like a copular/nominalizing element in poetry (here effectively forming 'beauty'). پای and پر are nouns 'foot' and 'feather/plume' listed as further attributes. The whole phrase enumerates martial and aesthetic qualities.
segments.s500-g237: ز is the poetic form of از meaning 'from/about'. کار is 'deed(s) / affair(s)'. زهشتان appears here as a single token in the manuscript but does not match the provided sense list (its meaning in context seems to be tied to 'your [bad] deeds' or an adjectival element), so it is marked -1. بپرسم is the first-person singular present/subjunctive of پرسیدن (بپرسم = 'I will ask / let me ask') and نهان is an adverb/adjective meaning 'secretly / hidden', modifying the asking.
segments.s500-g238: که introduces the indirect question or clause 'that / how'. بی‌مرد is a compound meaning 'without a husband' (بی- = without, مرد = husband/man). زن is 'woman'. چون here is an interrogative/adverb meaning 'how' (in the sense 'in what manner / what is she like'). بود is the past or stative form of بودن ('was / is'), and در جهان is the locative phrase 'in the world'. Together they ask how a husbandless woman exists/lives in the world.
segments.s500-g239: اگر is the conditional 'if'. مرگ is 'death'. باشد is the subjunctive/present form of بودن used as 'there is / be'. فزونی is a noun meaning 'increase / excess' (here 'abundance/excess'). ز is the poetic form of از meaning 'from'. کی is the interrogative 'who'. ست is the copula 'is' used to close the question ('from whom is it?'). Grammatically this is an if-clause asking the source/cause of an excess of death.
segments.s500-g240: به is a prepositional/purpose marker here ('to / let me') prefacing the verb. بینم is the first-person singular subjunctive/present of دیدن meaning 'let me see / I will see'. که introduces the indirect question 'what'. فرجام is the noun 'end / outcome'. این کار is 'this affair / this matter'. چی(ست) is the interrogative 'what' and ست is the copula 'is' — together forming the indirect question 'what is the outcome of this affair?'.
